title: Monsoon Sessions: Ep Collective Live
synopsis: Shot across one rain-soaked night at a warehouse space off Old Airport Road, this session film follows Ep Collective through a single unbroken take of their new single. The brief asked for texture over polish: handheld camera, practical lighting, the room's own reverb left in the mix. Grading leaned into the greens and ambers already present in the space rather than correcting toward a neutral palette.

title: Namma Metro: The City in Motion
synopsis: A 90-second brand film commissioned for the opening of the Yellow Line, built around commuters rather than infrastructure: a tailor catching the first train of the day, two students revising for an exam on the platform, a grandmother carrying idli boxes to her daughter's house across town. The metro itself appears mostly in reflections and passing frames, present but never the subject.

title: Chai Stalls of Old Bengaluru
synopsis: A self-initiated short documentary tracing four chai stalls that have run continuously since before the IT boom, filmed over three mornings in Malleswaram, Basavanagudi, Shivajinagar, and Fraser Town. No narration; the film lets pouring, glass-clinking, and half-heard counter conversations carry the structure. Made on our own time between commissioned work, mostly to keep the crew's eyes sharp between briefs.
